findings.title: Arbitrary CPI — untrusted program substitution
findings.description: The instruction program id is taken directly from caller-controlled token_program AccountInfo and never constrained to the SPL Token program. A malicious program can be supplied and invoked with signer privileges, enabling arbitrary logic during the CPI.
findings.impact: Full loss of tokens held in accounts that the authority can sign for; attacker-controlled SBF code executes under the victim's signer privileges.
findings.recommendation: Constrain token_program to the canonical SPL Token program using Program<'info, Token> or require_keys_eq!(token_program.key(), anchor_spl::token::ID) before invoke, and prefer Anchor CPI helpers over manual invoke.
